active.name: montezuma_near_death_priority_boost
active.description: Boost replay priority for near_death transitions in Montezuma where disagreement spikes.
active.llm_notes: Montezuma shows disagree_near_terminal_rate=0.7436 with mean_entropy=2.3881 and high_conf_disagree_rate=0.3377 over n_steps=267719. Increase sampling of survival-critical transitions to reduce terminal divergence.

active.name: montezuma_imitation_phase_extend_7000
active.description: Extend imitation-only phase for Montezuma before RL takeover.
active.llm_notes: Despite alignment_rate=0.6110, Montezuma remains highly uncertain (mean_entropy=2.3881) and reward_on_disagree (0.5857) exceeds reward_on_agree (0.0520), indicating unstable policy grounding. Extend imitation phase to stabilize action model.

active.name: frogger_high_conf_disagree_boost
active.description: Prioritize high-confidence disagreement samples in Frogger to correct confident wrong habits.
active.llm_notes: Frogger has alignment_rate=0.0289 with high_conf_disagree_rate=0.9711 and disagree_near_terminal_rate=1.0 across n_steps=67771. The agent is confidently wrong at death-adjacent states, so oversample high_conf_disagree transitions.

active.name: breakout_disagree_priority_boost
active.description: Increase replay weight for disagreement transitions in Breakout to improve imitation alignment.
active.llm_notes: Breakout has moderate alignment_rate=0.3691 with high_conf_disagree_rate=0.6309 and disagree_near_terminal_rate=0.5714 over n_steps=12453. A lighter disagreement boost can test whether mismatch reduction improves reward consistency (reward_on_agree=0.0100 vs reward_on_disagree=0.0074).

active.name: asterix_high_conf_disagree_boost
active.description: Boost replay priority for high-confidence disagreements so the agent relearns confident-but-wrong habits from human demonstrations.
active.llm_notes: Asterix shows alignment_rate=0.0292 with high_conf_disagree_rate=0.9708 and disagree_near_terminal_rate=0.8636, so confident disagreement dominates and should be oversampled.

active.name: asterix_imitation_phase_extend_8000
active.description: Extend imitation-only warmup to stabilize policy before RL takeover in a high-entropy, low-alignment regime.
active.llm_notes: Mean entropy is 2.0533 (>2.0) while alignment is only 0.0292, indicating the policy remains uncertain and poorly aligned; longer imitation should improve backbone action priors.

active.name: montezuma_near_death_priority_boost_v2
active.description: Increase near-death replay emphasis to target persistent disagreement in survival-critical moments.
active.llm_notes: Montezuma still has disagree_near_terminal_rate=0.7436 and mean_entropy=2.3881 despite existing ops, with eval runs stuck at max_room=1 and total_reward=0.0, so stronger near-death sampling is warranted.

active.name: centipede_near_death_priority_boost
active.description: Boost replay priority on near-death transitions to correct catastrophic divergence in survival-critical moments.
active.llm_notes: Centipede has alignment_rate=0.0003, high_conf_disagree_rate=0.9997, and disagree_near_terminal_rate=1.0 with mean_entropy=2.0995 across 13,863 steps, so survival-critical disagreement should be heavily oversampled.

active.name: defender_imitation_phase_extend_10000
active.description: Extend imitation-only warmup to stabilize policy priors before RL takeover in an extreme low-alignment regime.
active.llm_notes: Defender shows alignment_rate=0.0434 with mean_entropy=2.0306 and high_conf_disagree_rate=0.9566, while reward_on_agree=16.8449 greatly exceeds reward_on_disagree=1.1278, indicating human behavior is valuable but underlearned.

active.name: montezuma_imitation_phase_extend_10000
active.description: Increase Montezuma imitation-only warmup further to address persistent high-entropy uncertainty and stagnant eval progression.
active.llm_notes: Montezuma still has mean_entropy=2.3881 with disagree_near_terminal_rate=0.7436 and eval episodes stuck at max_room=1 with total_reward=0.0, so 7000 imitation steps appear insufficient for stable behavior grounding.

active.name: montezuma_subgoal_shaped_reward
active.description: Inject shaped rewards for 7 RAM-based sub-goals in Montezuma stage 1: ladder_reached (+0.5), ladder_descended (+1.0), platform_run_right (+1.5), rope_zone (+2.5), key_platform (+4.0), key_collected (+10.0), room_advanced (+20.0). Each fires once per episode on first attainment.
active.llm_notes: disagree_near_terminal_rate=0.74 and game_reward=0.0 across all 10 eval episodes confirms the agent has no gradient signal. All episodes stuck in room 1 (start_room) losing lives from 5 to 0. Sparse +100 key reward unreachable without sub-goal scaffolding. Stage 1 spatial layout anchored to calibrated RAM[42]=player_x, RAM[43]=player_y, RAM[3]=room.

active.name: montezuma_novelty_count_bonus
active.description: Count-based intrinsic novelty reward: bonus = 0.3 / sqrt(visit_count) for each new (room, x//8, y//8) cell. Decays as cells are revisited. Encourages spatial exploration without losing lives.
active.llm_notes: eval trajectory snapshots show agent oscillating at x=77-109 y=148-235 (room 1) across ALL 10 episodes with novelty_cells never growing. Count-based bonus creates gradient signal for lateral movement without requiring the sparse +100 key reward. Bin size 8 gives 20x28 cells per room = 560 unique states per room.

active.name: montezuma_amygdala_trajectory_switch
active.description: When any episode beats the best-room record, immediately inject all its transitions into the replay buffer at 10x priority and persist the trajectory as the new survivor. Exploits Montezuma determinism: the same action sequence from the same seed reproduces identically.
active.llm_notes: Montezuma is fully deterministic from a fixed seed. ep3 shows total_steps=410 (died fast) vs ep0-2 at 27000 steps — the variance is all about dying early, not advancing. Once a trajectory reaches room 2+, replaying those transitions at 10x drowns out the loss-inducing transitions and biases future sampling toward the winning path. This is the Go-Explore insight applied locally.

active.name: montezuma_decoder_key_dual_byte
active.description: Fix key_collected decoder: require BOTH RAM[56]==0xFF AND bit1 of RAM[65] set. The single-byte check caused false positives (key_collected=True at step 0) when ALE seeds initialize RAM[56] to non-zero. Confirmed from live RAM log: both bytes change simultaneously at reward=+100.
active.llm_notes: eval brief shows key_collected=True at step=0 across all 10 episodes and key_held_raw in items_raw=15 at game start. RAM reward log (ses_6589e0c2) confirms: at key pickup, ram_before[56]=0 AND ram_before[65]=0, ram_after[56]=255 AND ram_after[65]=2. Starting items_raw=15 (RAM[65]=15 at init) means the OR-based single check was always triggering. Dual-AND gate fixes this.

active.name: montezuma_frontier_recovery_curriculum
active.description: Montezuma shows collapse to zero recent reward despite long episodes; bias training toward frontier states, recoverable trajectories, and key-door completion micro-goals.
active.llm_notes: 300 episodes with recent20_mean=0.0 indicates sparse-reward starvation. Increase exploration persistence and shorten dream interval to improve planning frequency.

active.name: breakout_stability_then_speed
active.description: Breakout is stochastic+dense with low but nonzero reward; prioritize consistency before high-reward streak chasing.
active.llm_notes: 500 episodes, recent20_mean=0.8 and flat trend suggest noisy local policy. Slightly lower advisory use and longer horizon should reduce oscillation.

active.name: frogger_lane_phase_model
active.description: Frogger maintains moderate reward but plateaus; introduce lane-phase timing abstractions and selective risk pulses near completion states.
active.llm_notes: 500 episodes, recent20_mean above global mean but trend still flat. Improve temporal abstraction rather than raw exploration magnitude.

active.name: cartpole_relearn_foundation
active.description: CartPole performance is far below expected solved regime, indicating value-estimation instability; re-stabilize baseline control objective.
active.llm_notes: 1200 episodes with mean~13 implies core control policy has not consolidated. Treat as diagnostic anchor task and repair before trusting transfer metrics.

active.name: instrumentation_check_dreamer_reliance_lock
active.description: Cross-game diagnostics show dreamer_reliance fixed at 0.5 in all games; verify whether adaptive advisory calibration is disabled or clipped.
active.llm_notes: No PRINCIPLE ops emitted: the strongest apparent cross-game pattern is an instrumentation confound (constant reliance=0.5), which can invalidate causal conclusions.

active.name: montezuma_frontier_recovery_curriculum
active.description: Montezuma shows collapse to zero recent reward despite long episodes; bias training toward frontier states, recoverable trajectories, and key-door completion micro-goals.
active.llm_notes: 300 episodes with recent20_mean=0.0 indicates sparse-reward starvation. Increase exploration persistence and shorten dream interval to improve planning frequency.

active.llm_notes: 500 episodes, recent20_mean=0.8 and flat trend suggest noisy local policy. Slightly lower advisory use and longer horizon should reduce oscillation.

active.llm_notes: 500 episodes, recent20_mean above global mean but trend still flat. Improve temporal abstraction rather than raw exploration magnitude.

active.description: CartPole performance is far below expected solved regime, indicating value-estimation instability; re-stabilize baseline control objective.
active.llm_notes: 1200 episodes with mean~13 implies core control policy has not consolidated. Treat as diagnostic anchor task and repair before trusting transfer metrics.

active.name: montezuma_near_death_priority_boost_v3
active.description: Increase replay priority for Montezuma near-death disagreements to force repeated learning on survival-critical transitions.
active.llm_notes: Montezuma disagree_near_terminal_rate is 0.8519 and mean_entropy is 2.4109, indicating persistent uncertainty exactly where deaths happen; stronger oversampling should improve survival alignment.

active.name: montezuma_high_entropy_phase_extend_12000
active.description: Extend Montezuma imitation phase to 12k steps to reduce broad policy uncertainty before RL dominates updates.
active.llm_notes: Montezuma mean_entropy=2.4109 is well above the 2.0 uncertainty threshold while alignment_rate is only 0.4094, so a longer imitation warm-up should stabilize human-strategy features.

active.name: asterix_high_conf_disagree_boost_v2
active.description: Increase priority for Asterix high-confidence disagreements so confidently wrong habits are corrected faster.
active.llm_notes: Asterix has alignment_rate=0.0292 with high_conf_disagree_rate=0.9708 and disagree_near_terminal_rate=0.8636, a clear confidently-wrong regime requiring targeted replay pressure.

active.name: centipede_high_conf_disagree_boost_v2
active.description: Apply aggressive replay boost on Centipede high-confidence disagreements to break entrenched wrong policy loops.
active.llm_notes: Centipede shows alignment_rate=0.0003 and high_conf_disagree_rate=0.9997 with mean_entropy=2.0995, so nearly all confident actions diverge from demonstrations and should be heavily reweighted.

active.name: asterix_imitation_phase_extend_12000
active.description: Extend imitation-only warmup to 12000 steps for Asterix to reduce globally high uncertainty before RL dominates updates.
active.llm_notes: Asterix has alignment_rate=0.0292, high_conf_disagree_rate=0.9708, and mean_entropy=2.0533, indicating the policy is both uncertain and confidently misaligned. Extending the imitation phase beyond 8000 should improve representation grounding.

active.name: frogger_near_death_priority_boost_v1
active.description: Boost replay priority for near-death transitions in Frogger so survival-critical human moves are oversampled.
active.llm_notes: Frogger shows alignment_rate=0.0289 with high_conf_disagree_rate=0.9711 and disagree_near_terminal_rate=1.0, so divergence is worst at survival-critical states. A 3x near_death boost directly targets that gap.

active.name: riverraid_noop_imitation_downweight
active.description: Reduce imitation pressure on NOOP in Riverraid to avoid overfitting passive human segments when disagreement actions are yielding higher reward.
active.llm_notes: Riverraid has low alignment_rate=0.0264 and high_conf_disagree_rate=0.9736, but reward_on_disagree=1.3712 exceeds reward_on_agree=0.6696 while NOOP dominates both human and disagreement counts (12245). Downweighting NOOP imitation tests whether agent reward gains are being suppressed by passive imitation.

active.name: montezuma_room_boundary_priority_v1
active.description: Boost replay priority at room-boundary transitions in Montezuma to force learning of crossing behaviors out of the start room.
active.llm_notes: Montezuma alignment_rate=0.3924 with mean_entropy=2.434 and disagree_near_terminal_rate=0.8506, while eval episodes remain stuck at max_room=1 with key_collected_any=false. Oversampling boundary transitions targets the no-progression bottleneck directly.

active.name: montezuma_high_entropy_phase_extend_16000
active.description: Extend Montezuma imitation-only warmup to 16k steps to reduce persistent high-entropy action uncertainty before RL updates dominate.
active.llm_notes: Mean entropy remains very high at 2.434 (>2.0 uncertainty threshold) and latest eval trajectories repeatedly show q_entropy around 2.89 with no room progression beyond room 1. A longer imitation phase tests whether representation grounding is still insufficient at 12k.

active.name: defender_high_conf_disagree_boost_v1
active.description: Aggressively prioritize high-confidence disagreements in Defender to correct confidently wrong habits where human actions are much more rewarding.
active.llm_notes: Defender has alignment_rate=0.0434, high_conf_disagree_rate=0.9566, and disagree_near_terminal_rate=1.0; importantly reward_on_agree=16.8449 far exceeds reward_on_disagree=1.1278, indicating human actions are strongly superior and misaligned confident choices should be heavily replayed.
